text_clean:
jon jones has a history of failed drug tests throughout his ufc career  here are some of the key instances  

january 2015  tested positive for cocaine metabolites ahead of ufc 182  but was allowed to compete as cocaine is not banned out of competition by the world anti doping agency  wada 

july 2016  failed a drug test for two banned substances  clomiphene and letrozole  before ufc 200  leading to a one year suspension and being pulled from the event  usada determined he did not knowingly ingest the substances  finding they resulted from a contaminated supplement 

august 2017  tested positive for the anabolic steroid turinabol after his ufc 214 fight against daniel cormier  this resulted in his win being overturned to a no contest  another stripping of his title  and a 15 month suspension  usada again stated he didn t intentionally cheat but was given the 15 month ban due to  substantial assistance  in other anti doping cases 

december 2018  a trace amount of turinabol  the same substance found in his 2017 test  was detected in a pre fight drug test  however  based on revised usada policy and the low level detected  it was concluded this was a  pulsing effect  from the previous positive test and not a new violation
